title: Psammopyrum fontqueri
intro: Psammopyrum fontqueri, also known as Fontquer's sand-grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Psammopyrum fontqueri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Psammopyrum fontqueri is classified within the family Poaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, one of the seven subfamilies within the Poaceae family. The genus Psammopyrum is a small group of plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. Psammopyrum fontqueri is a species within this genus, and its classification also places it within the tribe Poeae. Further classification of Psammopyrum fontqueri includes its placement within the subtribe Poinae.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.
